differential_diagnoses: Pyroglutamic acidosis (5-oxoprolinuria) secondary to chronic acetaminophen and malnutrition
Acute kidney injury with uremic encephalopathy
next_steps: Measure arterial blood gas (ABG) with electrolytes to calculate the anion gap
Obtain serum lactate, beta-hydroxybutyrate, and acetaminophen levels
Perform urine organic acid analysis (specifically for 5-oxoproline)
Evaluate renal function and serum metformin levels
Discontinue metformin and all offending nephrotoxic or metabolic-stressing agents
Consider continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT) if severe metabolic acidosis persists
Obtain head CT to rule out structural intracranial pathology
